senses.gloss: April (Solar calendar used in Palestine, Syria, Lebanon, Jordan, and Iraq)

etymology.text:
Etymology tree
Akkadian 𒌚𒁈 (nisannum)bor.
Aramaic נִיסָן (nīsān)
Classical Syriac ܢܝܣܢ (nīsān)bor.
Arabic نيسان
Borrowed from Classical Syriac ܢܝܣܢ (nīsān), from Aramaic נִיסָן (nīsān), borrowed from Akkadian 𒌚𒁈 (nisannum).
Cognate with Hebrew נִיסָן (nisan, “Nisan, the first month of the Hebrew lunisolar calendar”).

body.text: ] It is [said to be] a property of the water of its rain, that dough kneaded with it ferments without other treatment. (TA.)
